idea.title: Math Computation and Visualization Demo
idea.problem: Demonstrate basic mathematical computation and data visualization pipeline
idea.hypothesisStatement: A simple pipeline computing basic statistics and generating charts
idea.keyInsight: Validating the full FAROS Agent execution pipeline end to end
idea.proposedMethod: Three-stage pipeline: Stage 1 does basic arithmetic, Stage 2 generates random data and computes statistics, Stage 3 creates matplotlib visualizations.

background.summary: Demo project for testing the FAROS autonomous agent pipeline with simple math operations.

stages.title: Stage 1: Basic Arithmetic Computation
stages.desc: Write Python code to perform basic arithmetic operations: addition, multiplication, and generate a simple number sequence.

steps.title: Arithmetic Operations
steps.desc: Write a Python script that: (1) computes sum of numbers 1 to 100, (2) computes product of numbers 1 to 10, (3) generates a Fibonacci sequence of length 15. Save results to a JSON file.
steps.method: Write and execute a Python script using basic arithmetic and json modules
steps.codeHints: Use basic Python math operations. No external libraries needed beyond json.

steps.title: Prime Number Finder
steps.desc: Write a Python script that finds all prime numbers between 2 and 200 using the Sieve of Eratosthenes algorithm. Save the list of primes to a JSON file.

outputs.name: primes.json
outputs.desc: List of prime numbers up to 200

steps.codeHints: Use a boolean list to mark non-primes. Time complexity O(n log log n).

stages.title: Stage 2: Data Generation and Statistics
stages.desc: Generate random data samples and compute basic statistical measures using the standard library.

steps.title: Statistical Data Generation
steps.desc: Write a Python script that: (1) generates 1000 random numbers from a normal distribution (mean=50, std=15), (2) computes mean, median, standard deviation, min, max, (3) saves all statistics to a JSON file. Use only the built-in random and statistics modules.
steps.method: Use random.gauss() and statistics module for computation
steps.codeHints: Use random.gauss(50, 15) for normal distribution. Use statistics.mean(), statistics.median(), statistics.stdev().

steps.title: Data Distribution Analysis
steps.desc: Read the statistics from step-2-1. Create a frequency histogram by binning data into 10 equal-width bins. Compute and save the bin counts and bin edges to a JSON file.
steps.method: Compute histogram bins manually using min/max range

expected.desc: All 1000 data points assigned to bins

steps.codeHints: Generate 1000 new random data points. Compute min/max, create 10 equal-width bins, count points in each bin.

stages.title: Stage 3: Visualization and Report
stages.desc: Generate visualizations from the computed data and produce a summary report.

steps.title: Data Visualization with Matplotlib
steps.desc: Write a Python script that: (1) generates 500 random data points, (2) creates a histogram chart using matplotlib, (3) creates a scatter plot of (x, y=x+noise), (4) saves both charts as PNG files. Install matplotlib if needed.
steps.method: Use matplotlib.pyplot to create publication-quality charts
steps.codeHints: pip install matplotlib if not available. Use plt.hist() and plt.scatter(). Save with plt.savefig().

steps.title: Summary Report Generation
steps.desc: Write a Python script that generates a summary markdown report (RESEARCH_REPORT.md) describing all computations performed, results obtained, and charts generated. Include the actual computed values from earlier steps if available.
steps.method: Generate a structured markdown report with sections

expected.desc: Report should have at least 4 sections

steps.codeHints: Write a markdown file with sections: Introduction, Methods, Results, Conclusion. Use computed values from the current workspace.
